text:
Market today: Risk-off tone with a tech-specific bite. Chip stocks getting hit hard (Micron -6%, ON Semi worst day since 2020), AI financing fears are spreading after Oracle's brutal week, and Kashkari floating a rate hike kills the 'Fed pivot' narrative that's been propping up growth names. This is exactly the environment that hurts your book.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and already on SELL. The chip selloff hitting Micron and ON Semi today will bleed into AVGO sentiment; watch that $336 stop hard. PLTR is down 13% on SELL status too — no specific catalyst today but tech rotation INTO healthcare (away from your names) is a real headwind. SPAL is down nearly 17% with decay risk baked in as a 2x leveraged ETF — on margin, that's a dangerous combo. These three deserve eyes today. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: (1) How SMH closes — it's your only green position and a canary for chips broadly. (2) Any Fed follow-up commentary hardening the hike view.

Bottom line: Your SELL-flagged names are bleeding in the exact macro/sector storm designed to hurt them — protect capital at those stops.
